signals.title: USD — CPI Surprise + Fed Governor Confirmation
signals.text: USD DXY at 98.26 following a 'hot' US CPI report and the Senate confirmation of Kevin Warsh to the Fed Board. Fed's Goolsbee notes inflation is moving the 'wrong way,' reinforcing the Fed 3.75% [ON HOLD] stance against immediate easing. US 10Y yields testing 4.463% as the curve enters a BEAR STEEPENER regime (2s10s +56.3bp). Higher-than-expected inflation and a hawkish-leaning Governor confirmation provide structural support for USD carry advantages, specifically the +175bp spread vs EUR and +300bp vs JPY.
signals.watch_level: Fed Chair vote on Wednesday for confirmation of policy trajectory.

signals.title: AUD/JPY — G8 Bilateral Theme + Carry
signals.text: AUD/JPY at 114.052 as the G8 leader-to-laggard spread reaches 1.055%. AUD outperformance (+0.530% avg) is driven by its status as the highest-yielding G8 currency (RBA 4.35% [HIKING]), while JPY remains the laggard (-0.524% avg) amid a +360bp carry deficit. Despite WTI Oil rallying +4.39% to $102.4 and Hormuz tensions introducing geopolitical risk, the carry trade remains dominant while VIX holds at 18.0 (normal volatility). Retail sentiment is 84% short, diverging from the institutional AUD COT LF net long +58,994, signaling a squeeze if 115.00 is tested.
signals.watch_level: VIX break above 25 to invalidate carry-first regime.

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence + RBNZ
signals.text: NZD/USD COT LF net short -19,294 (long% 18.5%) shows speculators are heavily positioned against the kiwi despite a hawkish RBNZ market bias (↑ Hike). With the RBNZ meeting on 27 May, a delivery of a hike or hawkish guidance creates significant short-squeeze risk for the crowded spec short. USD strength from the +150bp carry advantage and hot CPI is currently capping gains at 0.5956, but the positioning extreme (long% < 20%) suggests limited room for further downside without a shift in RBNZ expectations.
signals.watch_level: RBNZ meeting on 27 May for policy confirmation.

signals.title: GBP/USD — Technical Breakdown + Political Risk
signals.text: GBP/USD at 1.3542, down -0.49% intraday as sellers take control following political instability surrounding PM Keir Starmer. Despite a neutral carry profile (Fed 3.75% vs BoE 3.75%), the pair is the G8's biggest mover today. COT positioning remains net long +31,244, suggesting the current move is a liquidation of stale longs rather than a fresh short cycle. Support at 1.3500 is the immediate focus as the dollar bid intensifies.
